sources.name: DfE
sources.dataset: Education and Skills Funding Agency — Capital Programmes

metadata.methodology: Pupils in overcrowded schools are those attending schools where pupil numbers exceed the school's net capacity as reported in the DfE School Capacity (SCAP) survey. Secondary over-capacity percentage is the proportion of state-funded secondary schools with pupils on roll exceeding net capacity.
knownIssues: Net capacity is a DfE-defined measure based on usable teaching space — schools may use different definitions locally.
Temporary buildings count toward net capacity if approved, potentially understating true overcrowding in some schools.
